practicals.title: Laboratory Activities from Prescribed Books
practicals.details: Perform lab activities as prescribed in 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX' and 'Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage'. Topics include geometric constructions, mensuration experiments, and statistical data analysis.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to visualize irrational numbers.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to model patterns in sequences.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities using Geometric Models
activities.details: Use algebra tiles or geometric models to visualise factorisation and identities.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation on Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Sulbasutras
activities.details: Construct a square with a given side as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for 4-gons
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as Dharmachakra or Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Experiment
activities.details: Explore the classical problem of squaring a rectangle using methods from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret real-life data using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Random Experiment Simulation
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation and pattern recognition for random experiments.

projects.title: Mathematical Modelling of Linear Growth/Decay
projects.details: Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials in practical contexts.

projects.title: Fractal Analysis using Geometric Progressions
projects.details: Analyse attributes of fractals using properties of geometric progressions.

projects.title: Investigation of Circle Applications in Daily Life
projects.details: Research how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.

projects.title: Historical Approximations to Pi Research Project
projects.details: Investigate historical approximations of pi from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi.

projects.title: Real-life 3D Shape Recognition Survey
projects.details: Recognise cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, and spheres in real-life situations.

projects.title: Decision Making using Statistical Measures
projects.details: Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse data for decision-making based on mean, median, and mode.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding or solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge.
Examine information, present judgments based on criteria, or compile new patterns/solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with specific focus on logical reasoning, problem-solving, visualisation, mathematical modelling, and computational thinking.
No single competency is assigned a fixed mark weight; assessment evaluates the application of these competencies within each unit's context.
internal_assessment: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment: 5 + 5 Marks (Total 10)

practicals.title: Laboratory Activities from Prescribed Books
practicals.details: Perform lab activities as prescribed in 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX' and 'Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage'. Topics include geometric constructions, mensuration experiments, and statistical data analysis.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to visualize irrational numbers.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to model patterns in sequences.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities using Geometric Models
activities.details: Use algebra tiles or geometric models to visualise factorisation and identities.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation on Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Sulbasutras
activities.details: Construct a square with a given side as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for 4-gons
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as Dharmachakra or Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Experiment
activities.details: Explore the classical problem of squaring a rectangle using methods from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret real-life data using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Random Experiment Simulation
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation and pattern recognition for random experiments.

projects.title: Mathematical Modelling of Linear Growth/Decay
projects.details: Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials in practical contexts.

projects.title: Fractal Analysis using Geometric Progressions
projects.details: Analyse attributes of fractals using properties of geometric progressions.

projects.title: Investigation of Circle Applications in Daily Life
projects.details: Research how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.

projects.title: Historical Approximations to Pi Research Project
projects.details: Investigate historical approximations of pi from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi.

projects.title: Real-life 3D Shape Recognition Survey
projects.details: Recognise cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, and spheres in real-life situations.

projects.title: Decision Making using Statistical Measures
projects.details: Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse data for decision-making based on mean, median, and mode.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding or solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge.
Examine information, present judgments based on criteria, or compile new patterns/solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with specific focus on logical reasoning, problem-solving, visualisation, mathematical modelling, and computational thinking.
No single competency is assigned a fixed mark weight; assessment evaluates the application of these competencies within each unit's context.
internal_assessment: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment: 5 + 5 Marks (Total 10)

practical_assessment.description: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)

practicals.title: Laboratory Activities from Prescribed Books
practicals.details: Perform lab activities as prescribed in 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX' and 'Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage'. Topics include geometric constructions, mensuration experiments, and statistical data analysis.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to visualize irrational numbers.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to model patterns in sequences.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities using Geometric Models
activities.details: Use algebra tiles or geometric models to visualise factorisation and identities.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation on Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Sulbasutras
activities.details: Construct a square with a given side as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for 4-gons
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as Dharmachakra or Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Experiment
activities.details: Explore the classical problem of squaring a rectangle using methods from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret real-life data using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Random Experiment Simulation
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation and pattern recognition for random experiments.

projects.title: Mathematical Modelling of Linear Growth/Decay
projects.details: Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials in practical contexts.

projects.title: Fractal Analysis using Geometric Progressions
projects.details: Analyse attributes of fractals using properties of geometric progressions.

projects.title: Investigation of Circle Applications in Daily Life
projects.details: Research how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.

projects.title: Historical Approximations to Pi Research Project
projects.details: Investigate historical approximations of pi from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi.

projects.title: Real-life 3D Shape Recognition Survey
projects.details: Recognise cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, and spheres in real-life situations.

projects.title: Decision Making using Statistical Measures
projects.details: Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse data for decision-making based on mean, median, and mode.

competency_groups.title: Builds deductive and inductive logic for proofs
competencies: C-2.1 Understanding of powers (radical powers) and exponents.

competency_groups.title: Algebraic identities and modeling equations
competency_groups.description: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm.
competencies: C-3.1 States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm.

competency_groups.title: Geometric shapes analysis and arguments
competency_groups.description: Describes relationships including congruence of two-dimensional geometric shapes.

competency_groups.title: Derives formulae for areas and volumes
competency_groups.description: Visualises, represents, and calculates the area of a triangle using Heron's formula.

competency_groups.title: Data analysis using statistics and probability

competency_groups.title: Axiomatic structure appreciation
competency_groups.description: Proves mathematical statements using stated assumptions, axioms, postulates.

competency_groups.title: Visualisation and mathematical modelling skills
competency_groups.description: Models daily-life phenomena using representations such as graphs, tables, and equations.
competencies: C-8.1 Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions.

competency_groups.title: Computational thinking development
competencies: C-9.1 Decomposes a problem into sub-problems.

competency_groups.title: Appreciation of mathematicians' contributions
competency_groups.description: Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians in the field of Mathematics.
competencies: C-10.1 Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians (Indian and others) in the field of Mathematics.

competency_groups.title: Connections with other subjects
competency_groups.description: Applies mathematical knowledge to analyse problems in multiple subjects.
competencies: C-11.1 Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems or situations in multiple subjects across Science, Social Science, Visual Arts, Music, Vocational Education, and Sports.

Midpoint theorem and converse
Concurrency of medians (2:1 ratio)
Central symmetry in the context of parallelograms

subtopics: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: History of geometry

topics: Practical applications and uses of circles
key_concepts: Definition of a circle (centre, chord, diameter, radius)
Arc, segment, sector
Unique circle through three non-collinear points
Circumcircle and circumcentre location
Angle subtended by an arc at the centre vs. on circumference
Equal chords subtend equal angles
Perpendicular from centre bisects chord
Chord length vs distance from centre relationship
Diameter as longest chord
Angles in same segment are equal
Angle in a semicircle is right angle

subtopics: Area and Perimeter
Surface Area and Volume

topics: Perimeter and Area Introduction to Pi
Area of shapes: rectangles, parallelograms, and triangles
Area of the sector of a circle
Brahmagupta's formula for area of cyclic 4-gon
key_concepts: Definition of perimeter
Circumference-to-diameter ratio (Pi)
Irrationality of Pi
Length of an arc
Historical approximations to π
Heron's formula
Squaring a rectangle (Baudhayana)
Median divides triangle into equal area parts
Classical problem of squaring
Ancient civilisations approximations

topics: Surface areas and volumes of spheres (including hemispheres)
subtopics: Recognise cuboids and cubes in real-life situations. Compute the surface area and volume of a cuboid.
Explain how a cube is a 'special case' of a cuboid.
Describe a right circular cylinder using its radius and height. Compute the surface area and volume of a cylinder.
Recognise cones in daily life, and describe them using radius and height. Compute the surface area and volume of a cone.
Recognise a pyramid, identify its base and apex. Compute the surface area and volume of a pyramid.
Recognise spheres in real-life situations. Compute the surface area and volume of a sphere.
key_concepts: Heron's formula as a special case of Brahmagupta's formula
Notion of 'special case' and 'generalisation'
Computational thinking in geometry: breaking down shapes, step-by-step methods for perimeter/area, pattern recognition

subtopics: Statistics
Introduction to Probability
Graphical representation of data. Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data.
Measures of central tendency (weighted average). Read stacked bar graphs and 100% stacked bar graphs.
key_concepts: Computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life data
Decision-making using mean, median and mode

subtopics: The probability scale. Describe likelihood using the scale.
Empirical probability: Analysing statistical data and performing experiments.
Theoretical probability: Sample space and events. Define theoretical probability.
key_concepts: Representing probability through tree diagrams and tables
Computational thinking strategies (pattern recognition, simulation) for random experiments

practicals.title: Laboratory Activities from Prescribed Books
practicals.details: Perform lab activities as prescribed in 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX' and 'Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage'. Topics include geometric constructions, mensuration experiments, and statistical data analysis.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to visualize irrational numbers.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to model patterns in sequences.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities using Geometric Models
activities.details: Use algebra tiles or geometric models to visualise factorisation and identities.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation on Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Sulbasutras
activities.details: Construct a square with a given side as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for 4-gons
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as Dharmachakra or Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Experiment
activities.details: Explore the classical problem of squaring a rectangle using methods from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret real-life data using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Random Experiment Simulation
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation and pattern recognition for random experiments.

projects.title: Mathematical Modelling of Linear Growth/Decay
projects.details: Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials in practical contexts.

projects.title: Fractal Analysis using Geometric Progressions
projects.details: Analyse attributes of fractals using properties of geometric progressions.

projects.title: Investigation of Circle Applications in Daily Life
projects.details: Research how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.

projects.title: Historical Approximations to Pi Research Project
projects.details: Investigate historical approximations of pi from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi.

projects.title: Real-life 3D Shape Recognition Survey
projects.details: Recognise cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, and spheres in real-life situations.

projects.title: Decision Making using Statistical Measures
projects.details: Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse data for decision-making based on mean, median, and mode.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding or solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge.
Examine information, present judgments based on criteria, or compile new patterns/solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with specific focus on logical reasoning, problem-solving, visualisation, mathematical modelling, and computational thinking.
No single competency is assigned a fixed mark weight; assessment evaluates the application of these competencies within each unit's context.
internal_assessment: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment: 5 + 5 Marks (Total 10)

practical_assessment.description: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)
